bullets.text: Venezuela has been hit by a devastating 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude earthquake, with rescue teams searching for survivors amidst collapsed buildings and a rising death toll.
A severe heatwave is impacting Europe, with France reporting heat-related deaths among young people and temperatures in Germany expected to reach 40C.
France has confirmed its first case of Ebola in a doctor who worked in the Democratic Republic of Congo, with health officials tracing the patient's contacts.
The NT government continues to implement hardline school policies despite a decline in attendance, with just over half of all Aboriginal students showing up for school.
Channel Nine has confirmed that Today show host Karl Stefanovic will be leaving the network following a controversial podcast interview.
The Australian government has indicated that it may strengthen social media bans.
Apple has increased prices for its MacBook and iPad products by 20%, citing an AI-driven memory shortage, which led to a $263 billion loss in market capitalization for the iPhone maker.
New business formation is surging once again, following an initial jump in 2020 due to the pandemic's reorganization of work, shopping, and logistics.
US airline stocks have recovered from pandemic-era losses, a recovery attributed to easing oil prices following progress on a peace deal between the US and Iran.
